description: CCRL-Blitz-anchored opponent pool for absolute Sgurr calibration. Names must match fastchess PGN tags and anchors.txt exactly.
notes: Ratings are single-CPU CCRL Blitz (2'+1"), re-sourced from the live list on 2026-07-15.

SUPERSEDES pool-2026-07-A. An audit found every pool-A anchor inflated vs the current list by 12-50 Elo (mean ~31; Zahak-5.0 was 2763 vs 2726 today), because those values came from each engine's README rather than the live list. Every value here is re-sourced, so this scale is internally consistent but sits ~31 below pool-A's. Sgurr ratings measured on pool-B are therefore NOT comparable to the pool-A ledger rows: compare versions only within a single Ordo solve, which re-solves all accumulated PGNs onto one scale. Internal gaps between Sgurr versions are anchor-independent and unaffected.
Mixing fresh and stale anchor values would be worse than either alone: Ordo pins all anchors, so an inconsistent set fits a compromise, inflating residuals and error bars.

Four engine families (Blunder, Zahak, Weiss, Igel). Weiss and Igel were added to bracket Sgurr v5.0 from above -- the search update (+173 self-play SPRT) plus the gen6 net projects the strongest variant near the old pool's ceiling -- and to reduce family-specific style bias in the absolute scale.

Blunder-6.1.0 is dropped from this gauntlet roster (it scored 93% vs v4.0: no Elo signal) but is RETAINED in anchors.txt at 2105. anchors.txt and this roster serve different roles: this roster is who the new gauntlet plays, anchors.txt is who Ordo pins across ALL accumulated PGNs. Blunder-6.1.0 remains the only anchor below ~2500 and brackets the historical Sgurr classical (2398) and v1.0 (2407) rows via its existing games.

Blunder-7.2.0 is removed entirely, including from anchors.txt: it has no CCRL Blitz rating at any version (the list has 7.1.0 = 2387 and 7.4.0 = 2520, but no 7.2.0), so its former 2425 was a README figure and was never CCRL-anchored. It now floats as a free node in the Ordo solve, still connected through its historical games.

Binaries: Weiss uses the pext build (fast BMI2 on the i5-9400F, matching Sgurr's -march=native); Igel uses the x64_popcnt Windows build. All three added engines are classical-eval and need no external network file.
Pool is append-only: never remove or re-rate an engine within a pool_id -- create a new pool_id instead.
